meta_title: For Freelancers: client workflow to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Client Workflow to Solve No SaaS Knowledge
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap in SaaS knowledge with this professional freelancer workflow. Master Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver high-end apps with speed.
content.answer_block: Overcoming a lack of SaaS knowledge requires shifting from manual coding to an AI-augmented, modular architecture. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you transform complex requirements into production-ready features, ensuring you meet client deadlines while maintaining professional standards without needing years of experience.
content.intro:
The client stares at you, waiting for a SaaS solution you aren't entirely sure how to architect. The clock is ticking, the budget is fixed, and the fear of 'not knowing' is a heavy anchor dragging down your confidence. You aren't just building an app; you are building your reputation, and failure isn't an option in the high-stakes world of freelance development.

But here is the secret: the most successful builders don't know everything—they know how to execute. By standardizing your workflow with modern AI agents, you turn the unknown into a repeatable process. We are going to strip away the noise and give you a battle-tested blueprint to ship professional SaaS products, regardless of your starting experience level.

steps.title: Define the Primitive Requirements
steps.description: Before writing code, extract the core SaaS functionality from the client's vague brief to prevent scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze these client requirements and extract the core SaaS primitives (Auth, Database, CRUD, API). Output a structured JSON schema for these features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review the project requirements and generate a technical spec document for a MVP'.

steps.title: Scaffold the Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate, ensuring a scalable folder structure from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a production-ready Next.js SaaS boilerplate with Tailwind CSS, Supabase integration, and Stripe checkout flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project scaffolding using the Antigravity architecture patterns'.

steps.title: Implement Database Logic
steps.description: Define your data models to ensure the backend is robust and capable of supporting user-specific data isolation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create Drizzle ORM schemas for a multi-tenant SaaS application including Users, Organizations, and Subscription tiers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'npx drizzle-kit generate' && claude terminal 'npx drizzle-kit push'

steps.title: Build the Core Feature Set
steps.description: Iteratively build features using Claude Code to handle the heavy lifting of API endpoints and UI state management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Server Action for processing Stripe webhooks and updating the user subscription status in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the Stripe webhook handler in the /api/webhooks/stripe route'.

steps.title: Automated Quality Assurance
steps.description: Use AI agents to run regression tests on your codebase before the client demo to ensure zero-friction performance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of Playwright tests for the user sign-up flow and subscription upgrade process.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute end-to-end testing for critical path components and report any failures'.

pro_tips: Always treat your client's requirements as 'hypotheses'—use Antigravity to build small, testable prototypes before committing to a full stack.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write; a well-commented codebase is the best way to justify your premium rates.
Automate the deployment pipeline early; if it isn't deployed, it doesn't exist to the client.
Implement feature flags from the start to toggle unfinished modules during client reviews without breaking the build.
Maintain a 'Prompt Library' of your most successful Antigravity configurations to reuse across different client projects.
content.next_steps: After completing your first MVP, deploy to Vercel and set up a staging environment for live client feedback. Begin iterating based on real usage metrics.
content.conversion_block: Stop guessing and start shipping with a system designed for high-velocity freelancers. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
